title: cirsium spathulatum
intro: Cirsium spathulatum, also known as the western yarrow or nodding thistle, is a flowering plant native to the northwestern United States and southwestern British Columbia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and infections. The health benefits of Cirsium spathulatum include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, making it a popular choice among herbalists and natural health practitioners.
